speaker: Tina
line: Hey Nina! Today we're diving into my Top 5 everyday things

speaker: Tina
line: you didn't know had incredibly weird origins.

speaker: Nina
line: Weird origins? I'm skeptical. Most things seem pretty straightforward,

speaker: Nina
line: don't they?

speaker: Tina
line: You'd be surprised! Kicking off our list at number five:

speaker: Tina
line: high heels.

speaker: Nina
line: High heels? Don't they just make your feet hurt and make you

speaker: Nina
line: taller? What's weird about that?

speaker: Tina
line: Well, originally they were for Persian cavalry to keep their feet

speaker: Tina
line: in stirrups while shooting arrows.

speaker: Tina
line: Think of it like a horse wearing tiny stilts to get a better grip.

speaker: Tina
line: Not exactly a fashion statement back then!

speaker: Nina
line: Okay, that's genuinely unexpected. I didn't know that.

speaker: Tina
line: Next up at number four, Ketchup.

speaker: Nina
line: Ketchup? It's literally just tomatoes, vinegar, and sugar,

speaker: Nina
line: isn't it? What could be weird about that?

speaker: Tina
line: Our beloved condiment started as 'kê-tsiap,' a fermented fish sauce

speaker: Tina
line: from China with no tomatoes!

speaker: Tina
line: It's like finding out your favorite sweet candy bar started

speaker: Tina
line: as a savory beef jerky.

speaker: Nina
line: A fish sauce? That's a huge leap! My mind is a little blown.

speaker: Tina
line: Moving on to number three: Bubble Wrap.

speaker: Nina
line: Bubble wrap? That's just for popping and protecting packages,

speaker: Nina
line: isn't it? How's that weird?

speaker: Tina
line: It was originally invented to be textured wallpaper!

speaker: Tina
line: Imagine trying to sell a scratchy sweater as a luxurious blanket.

speaker: Tina
line: It just didn't work for its original intent.

speaker: Nina
line: Wallpaper? That's hilarious! I can't imagine a bubble-wrapped room.

speaker: Tina
line: Coming in hot at number two, the Rubber Duck.

speaker: Nina
line: Wait, rubber ducks? They're bath toys! What's weird about that,

speaker: Nina
line: Tina? That's just wrong!

speaker: Tina
line: Originally, they were solid, hard rubber chew toys, not floaty bath toys!

speaker: Tina
line: They were like the indestructible dog toys of their time, but for babies.

speaker: Tina
line: Then someone put them in water, and voilà!

speaker: Nina
line: Solid? So they just sank like a rock? That's not a bath toy at all!

speaker: Tina
line: And for our number one weird origin... Toilet Paper!

speaker: Nina
line: Toilet paper? Come on, that's just paper! You can't tell me

speaker: Nina
line: it was anything else.

speaker: Tina
line: It was first documented for Chinese emperors. But before soft rolls,

speaker: Tina
line: people used everything from corn cobs to communal sponges on sticks.

speaker: Tina
line: Imagine sharing a sponge on a stick. Suddenly, paper seems revolutionary!

speaker: Nina
line: Okay, okay, the sponge on a stick seals it. That's truly wild.

speaker: Nina
line: My mind's officially blown.

speaker: Tina
line: Glad I could expand your 'weird origins' horizons! Catch you next time!
